schema.appDescription: Calculate recommended life insurance cover amount based on income, liabilities, and dependents. Get an indicative annual premium estimate.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Term Insurance Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: How much term insurance cover do I need?
faq.a: A common rule: cover = 15–20× annual income. But the right cover = human life value (HLV). Calculate: (income × remaining working years) + all liabilities − existing assets. This ensures your family can repay debts and sustain their lifestyle without your income.

faq.q: What is the right age to buy term insurance?
faq.a: Buy term insurance as early as possible — premiums are lowest when young and healthy. A 25-year-old pays roughly half the premium of a 35-year-old for the same cover. Buy a long-tenure plan (30–35 years) to cover liabilities until retirement.

faq.q: Which is better — ₹1 crore or ₹2 crore term cover?
faq.a: For someone earning ₹10–15 lakh/year with a home loan, ₹1 crore is typically inadequate. ₹1.5–2 crore is recommended. Premium difference is small — ₹1 crore plan at 32 costs ₹10,000–₹12,000/year; ₹2 crore plan costs ₹18,000–₹22,000/year. The additional cover is worth the marginal premium.
No. All calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: 32-year-old earning ₹12 lakh with home loan
examples.text: Annual income ₹12 lakh, remaining working years 28, home loan ₹30 lakh, other assets ₹10 lakh. Recommended cover = (12,00,000 × 28) + 30,00,000 − 10,00,000 = ₹3,56,00,000. Round up to ₹3.5 crore. Estimated premium for ₹3.5 crore cover: ₹28,000–₹35,000/year.

examples.heading: Increasing cover vs flat cover
examples.text: Instead of ₹1 crore flat cover, consider ₹75 lakh increasing cover (grows 5%/year). Over 30 years, cover doubles to ₹3+ crore, matching inflation. Premium is similar but protection is far superior in later years when your income and liabilities are higher.
